reasons: EMERGENCY: diversity collapsed with frozen particles

turns.thought: The swarm has suffered a complete diversity collapse: normalized diversity is near zero, velocities are nearly frozen (73% zero), and particles are highly aligned. The optimization is stuck in a deceptive valley of the Rosenbrock function, far from the global optimum. This is an emergency situation requiring aggressive re-diversification. A reset of the worst-performing particles will inject new random positions and velocities, breaking the collapse and enabling escape from the local basin. I choose ratio=0.4 (above the recommended 0.35) to ensure sufficient diversity restoration, with reset_velocity=true to overcome velocity stagnation, and inertia_weight=1.1 to promote global exploration after the intervention.
turns.observation: Action 'reset_worst_particles' was applied. Reinitialized 12 weak particles to restore diversity. Best cost changed from 4.975175e+02 to 4.975175e+02 (delta=+0.000000e+00).
